title: Authentication, Authorization, RBAC, Service Accounts, Tokens, and Impersonation · Kubernetes Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release review compares fresh authentication, authorization, rbac, service accounts, tokens, and impersonation choices and accepts only answers tied to observable API, controller, workload, network, storage, or policy evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for least-privilege identity and authorization policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace TLS identity, authentication, user and group information, authorization, admission, validation, persistence, and audit for one request.
Separate external human users and groups, ServiceAccounts, node identities, workload identity federation, image pull identity, and bootstrap credentials.
Construct namespace-scoped Roles and RoleBindings or justified ClusterRoles and ClusterRoleBindings from verbs, resources, names, non-resource URLs, and subjects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain
steps.instruction:
Order the K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain orchestration evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain.
steps.why: Correct order keeps K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain, keep K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A quiz workload uses the default ServiceAccount, automounts a broad token, and receives wildcard cluster-admin through a group binding. Derive subject, verb, resource, namespace, token audience, impersonation, and least-privilege proof. Competency probe: Trace TLS identity, authentication, user and group information, authorization, admission, validation, persistence, and audit for one request.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A quiz workload uses the default ServiceAccount, automounts a broad token, and receives wildcard cluster-admin through a group binding. Derive subject, verb, resource, namespace, token audience, impersonation, and least-privilege proof. Competency probe: Trace TLS identity, authentication, user and group information, authorization, admission, validation, persistence, and audit for one request.

For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: RBAC authenticates users and admission decides whether they can connect to the API server.

options.text: Capture client and server versions, context, namespace, API discovery, current object state, ownership, and immutable release identity for the quiz K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain case
Predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, security, disruption, and cleanup effects for the quiz K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain case
Review the smallest manifest, Kustomize, Helm, policy, or runbook increment in the deterministic browser simulator for the quiz K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain case
Reject one failure, record repair and rollback, then name authorized disposable-cluster transfer evidence for the quiz K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain case
